mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate, particularly after a recession, often signals a contrarian buying opportunity as the market anticipates future economic recovery and job creation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical market bottoms near unemployment peaks
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains above 9.5% for 6 consecutive months and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative during this period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low federal funds rate) indicates strong consumer demand without monetary tightening, which is highly supportive of corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and low consumer price index in a post-recession environment allows the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL shows sustained disinflation (3-month average change < 0) and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts signals a recovery in the housing market and broader economic confidence, which translates into increased construction activity and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market as leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST growth exceeds 10% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such occurrences over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing and supply-side activity, reflecting increased business investment and demand, which typically precedes or accompanies positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive growth for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of these periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of positive GDP growth and a declining unemployment rate signifies a robust economic expansion where both output and labor markets are improving, creating a favorable environment for corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
failure_condition: If both GDP growth and unemployment decline conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance by the central bank, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, thereby supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.20% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations during this period.
